steps.title: Identify the sample problem
steps.content: Denise wants information about all households in the community, but she surveyed only families at a children's museum. That location is likely to overrepresent households with children and underrepresent households without children, so the sample does not represent the whole community well enough.

steps.title: Draw the required conclusion
steps.content: Because the sample was selected from a location connected to children, the sampling method is flawed and may produce a biased estimate of the mean number of children per household in the community. The answer is C.
